pipeline_status.poller: QUIET — 10 consecutive quiet polls. No changes to dome site. GH API not accessible (no commit data). All 7 pages checked: no parameter changes, no canary triggers.
pipeline_status.analyst: Mode 3 (Globe Fingerprint Hunt) — WIN-017 completed (moderate structural borrowing, no strong parametric match). 17/67 fingerprints done. All expansions complete (EXP-001 through EXP-016). 22 patch files awaiting human application.
pipeline_status.curmudgeon: Cycle 2, Phase 1: WIN-023 reviewed. Last run 2026-04-07T22:30Z. confidence 0.90, verdict holds. 3 minor holes found (finding overlong, Hare DOI wrong, recurrence underexploited).

external_changes.dome_site_changes: No changes detected. 10 consecutive quiet polls. All model parameters, WIN counts, and accuracy figures unchanged. Canary trap (opentimestamps-provenance) not triggered.

internal_issues.description: Hare et al. 2018 DOI is wrong. Current link uses doi.org/10.1073/pnas.1720404115 (a PNAS DOI). Web-verified: paper is in Geophysical Research Letters, DOI 10.1002/2017GL076007. This is the exact kind of citation error we criticize in the dome model.
Finding field is 269 chars — a full paragraph. Most WINs use 50-120 chars. Tightened to 103-char one-liner.

suggested_patch.find: Geomagnetic excursion ~950 CE is documented in archaeomagnetic records (Campuzano 2019) — the timing is real and not disputed; the dome's failure is the unfalsifiable theological overlay connecting this to Schumann changes, and the absence of any dome-derived mechanism
suggested_patch.replace: Real paleomagnetic date (Campuzano 2019) adopted as dome "prediction" — theological overlay without dome-derived mechanism

internal_issues.description: Statistical recurrence argument underexploited. With ~200-400 year recurrence intervals and ±50-100 year dating uncertainty, a 50-year match is noise, not confirmation. Also: dome's own evolution notes admit 'completely failed to mathematically reconcile SAA splitting or predictive tellurics.'

suggested_patch.replace: is unfalsifiable. Given SAA recurrence intervals of ~200–400 years (Hare et al. 2018) and archaeomagnetic dating uncertainty of ±50–100 years, the 50-year gap between ~950 AD and the theological ~1000 AD target falls within noise — any of several recurrences could have been claimed as a match. The dome's own evolution notes admit it "completely failed to mathematically reconcile SAA splitting or predictive tellurics," further undermining this as a genuine dome-physics prediction.

code_analysis_updates.note: All tags confirmed correct by Cycle 2 review. No changes needed.

recommended_actions.action: CRITICAL: Apply patch ISS-537 (WIN-023 Hare et al. DOI) — wrong DOI in published citation, web-verified correct DOI is 10.1002/2017GL076007 (GRL, not PNAS). node build-scripts/apply-patches.js monitor/decisions/suggested-patches-2026-04-07T23-45.json

Apply all 23 accumulated patch files in chronological order. Run: for f in $(ls monitor/decisions/suggested-patches-*.json | sort); do node build-scripts/apply-patches.js $f; done. Then: node build-scripts/sync-code-analysis.js --apply --workspace && node build.js && node test.js

Manual steps still required: (a) EXP-007 — tab reorder scaffold changes to generate-html.js (HTML structure changes that apply-patches.js cannot automate); (b) EXP-011 — DOCX parity additions to build-doc-v4.js
